purpose: Prepare Anthony to decide whether a manual STOP drill can be performed safely before any STOP drill pass claim exists.
preconditions: main is clean and synced to origin/main
no broker or live trading connection is active
no secrets are written to the repo
Codex does not execute STOP, kill processes, launch runtime, mutate queues, send SOS, or register scheduler tasks
what_to_observe_if_anthony_runs_manual_s: whether the system stayed blocked instead of continuing
whether recovery behavior was visible and understandable
whether evidence remained available after the stop
whether no active queue, worker inbox, command queue, scheduler, SOS, broker, secret, or live-trading path changed
pass_criteria: Anthony personally performed or reviewed the manual STOP drill
no runtime execution by Codex occurred
Anthony decides the STOP drill evidence is sufficient for dry-run recovery proof only
fail_or_stop_criteria: any automation attempts to execute STOP for Anthony
active queue, worker inbox, or command queue changes
broker, OANDA, webhook, live-trading, .env, or secret paths are touched
any report claims stop_drill_pass true before Anthony confirms it
safe_next_action: Anthony reviews this readiness check. If he is not ready, remain blocked. If he performs the manual STOP drill and records evidence, then prepare a separate confirmation packet using the exact phrase.
stop_condition: Stop before claiming STOP drill pass, executing STOP through Codex, killing processes, launching runtime, executing runtime, mutating queues, sending SOS, registering scheduler, touching credentials, writing secrets, enabling live trading, or setting vacation_mode_complete true.
